heading: Career Act; treatment, recovery, and workforce support grants
label: 290ee–8 Career Act; treatment, recovery, and workforce support grants
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Substance Use–Disorder Prevention that Promotes Opioid Recovery and Treatment for Patients and Communities Act, also known as the SUPPORT for Patients and Communities Act, and not as part of the Public Health Service Act which comprises this chapter.

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Secretary of Health and Human Services (referred to in this section as the “Secretary”), in consultation with the Secretary of Labor, shall continue or establish a program to support individuals in substance use disorder treatment and recovery to live independently and participate in the workforce.

children.heading: Grants authorized
children.label: b Grants authorized
children.text: In carrying out the activities under this section, the Secretary shall, on a competitive basis, award grants each for a period of not more than 5 years to entities to enable such entities to carry out evidence-based programs to help individuals in substance use disorder treatment and recovery to live independently and participate in the workforce. Such entities shall coordinate, as applicable, with Indian tribes or tribal organizations (as applicable), State boards and local boards (as defined in , lead State agencies with responsibility for a workforce investment activity (as defined in such section 3102), and State agencies responsible for carrying out substance use disorder prevention and treatment programs.section 3102 of title 29

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In awarding grants under this section, the Secretary shall give priority based on the State in which the entity is located. Priority shall be given among States according to a formula based on the average rates for calendar years 2018 through 2022 described in paragraph (2) and weighted as described in paragraph (3).

children.label: A
children.text: The highest age-adjusted average rates of drug overdose deaths for calendar years 2018 through 2022 based on data from the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, including, if necessary, provisional data for calendar year 2022.

children.label: B
children.text: The highest average rates of unemployment for calendar years 2018 through 2022 based on data provided by the Bureau of Labor Statistics.

children.label: C
children.text: The lowest average labor force participation rates for calendar years 2018 through 2022 based on data provided by the Bureau of Labor Statistics.

children.label: A
children.text: The rate described in paragraph (2)(A) shall be weighted 70 percent.

children.label: B
children.text: The rate described in paragraph (2)(B) shall be weighted 15 percent.

children.label: C
children.text: The rate described in paragraph (2)(C) shall be weighted 15 percent.

children.heading: Preference
children.label: d Preference
children.text: In awarding grants under this section, the Secretary shall give preference to entities located in areas within States with the greatest need, with such need based on the highest mortality rate related to substance use disorder.

children.heading: Eligible entity
children.label: 1 Eligible entity
children.text: The term “eligible entity” means an entity that offers treatment or recovery services for individuals with substance use disorders, and partners with one or more local or State stakeholders, which may include local employers, community organizations, the local workforce development board, local and State governments, and Indian Tribes or tribal organizations, to support recovery, independent living, and participation in the workforce.

children.heading: Indian Tribes; tribal organization
children.label: 2 Indian Tribes; tribal organization
children.text: The terms “Indian Tribe” and “tribal organization” have the meanings given the terms “Indian tribe” and “tribal organization” in .section 5304 of title 25

children.heading: State
children.label: 3 State
children.text: The term “State” includes only the several States and the District of Columbia.
identify gaps in the workforce due to the prevalence of substance use disorders;

in coordination with statewide employment and training activities, including coordination and alignment of activities carried out by entities provided grant funds under , help individuals in recovery from a substance use disorder transition into the workforce, including by providing career services, training services as described in paragraph (2) of , and related services described in section 3174(a)(3) of such title; andsection 3225a of title 29section 3174(c) of title 29
assist employers with informing their employees of the resources, such as resources related to substance use disorders that are available to their employees.

children.label: i
children.text: the development and strengthening of daily living skills; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the use of counseling, care coordination, and other services, as appropriate, to support recovery from substance use disorders.

children.label: B
children.text: Implement or utilize innovative technologies, which may include the use of telemedicine.

children.label: i
children.text: short-term prevocational training services; and

children.label: ii
children.text: training services that are directly linked to the employment opportunities in the local area or the planning region.

children.heading: Transportation services
children.label: 2 Transportation services
children.text: An entity receiving a grant under this section may use not more than 5 percent of the funds for providing transportation for individuals to participate in an activity supported by a grant under this section, which transportation shall be to or from a place of work or a place where the individual is receiving vocational education or job training services or receiving services directly linked to treatment of or recovery from a substance use disorder.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 3 Limitation
children.text: The Secretary may not require an entity to, or give priority to an entity that plans to, use the funds of a grant under this section for activities that are not specified in this subsection.

children.heading: Support for State strategy
children.label: h Support for State strategy
children.text: An eligible entity shall include in its application under subsection (f) information describing how the services and activities proposed in such application are aligned with the State, outlying area, or Tribal strategy, as applicable, for addressing issues described in such application and how such entity will coordinate with existing systems to deliver services as described in such application.

children.heading: Data reporting and program oversight
children.label: i Data reporting and program oversight

children.text: the programs and activities funded by the grant;

outcomes of the population of individuals with a substance use disorder the grantee served through activities described in subsection (g), which shall include employment and earnings outcomes described in subclauses (I) and (III) of with respect to the participation of such individuals with a substance use disorder in programs and activities funded by the grant under this section; andsection 3141(b)(2)(A)(i) of title 29
any other information that the Secretary may require for the purpose of ensuring that the grantee is complying with all of the requirements of the grant.

children.heading: Preliminary report
children.label: 1 Preliminary report
children.text: Not later than 2 years after the end of the first year of the grant period under this section for grants awarded prior to , the Secretary shall submit to Congress a preliminary report that analyzes reports submitted under subsection (i).December 1, 2025

children.label: A
children.text: a description of how the grant funding was used, including the number of individuals who received services under subsection (g)(1)(C) and an evaluation of the effectiveness of the activities conducted by the grantee with respect to outcomes of the population of individuals with substance use disorder who receive services from the grantee; and

children.label: B
children.text: recommendations related to best practices for health care professionals to support individuals in substance use disorder treatment or recovery to live independently and participate in the workforce.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: k Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated $12,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2026 through 2030 for purposes of carrying out this section.
